LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LOANME, INC.'S HEARING ON MOTION FOR AWARD OF POST-JUDGMENT ATTORNEYS FEES.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF LOANME, INC.'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF POST-JUDGMENT ATTORNEYS' FEES IS GRANTED IN PART. PARAGRAPH 9 OF THE FORBEARANCE AGREEMENT PLAINLY STATES THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO ATTORNEYS' FEES BECAUSE OF DEFENDANTS LOCAL TASTES OF THE CITY TOURS, LLC AND THOMAS MEDIN'S DEFAULT. HOWEVER, CCP SECTION 685.040 ONLY ENTITLES A JUDGMENT CREDITOR TO REASONABLE AND NECESSARY COSTS OF ENFORCING A JUDGMENT. DESPITE THE BROAD LANGUAGE OF PARAGRAPH 9 INDICATING A RIGHT TO RETROACTIVE FEES, THE FEES INCURRED PRIOR TO MAY 9, 2016 WERE NOT REASONABLE AND NECESSARY COSTS OF ENFORCING THE JUDGMENT BECAUSE THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT HAD NOT YET BEEN ENTERED. DEDUCTING THE ACTUAL COSTS INCURRED BEFORE THIS DATE, RATHER THAN THE INITIAL ATTORNEYS' FEES AWARD, RESULTS IN A NEW AWARD OF $4905.00. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/SRB)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Award Of Post-Judgment Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kenneth Shemwell, Esq. 619-546-9200 of the Law Offices of David Brody for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Thomas Lalanne, Esq. 415-434-1122 for Defendant Local Tastes of the City Tours.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF LOANME, INC.'S Hearing On Motion For Award Of Post-Judgment Attorneys Fees.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff LoanMe, Inc.'s motion for award of post-judgment attorneys' fees is granted in part. Paragraph 9 of the Forbearance Agreement plainly states that Plaintiff is entitled to attorneys' fees because of Defendants Local Tastes of the City Tours, LLC and Thomas Medin's default. However, CCP section 685.040 only entitles a judgment creditor to reasonable and necessary costs of enforcing a judgment. Despite the broad language of Paragraph 9 indicating a right to retroactive fees, the fees incurred prior to May 9, 2016 were not reasonable and necessary costs of enforcing the judgment because the default judgment had not yet been entered. Deducting the actual costs incurred before this date, rather than the initial attorneys' fees award, results in a new award of $4905.00.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
